content:
While training over the course of a semester to become an effective writing consultant, I learned a lot about how many different cultures approach arguments in different shapes and patterns. My writing consultant career helped me work in a micro way with students, and trained me to spot the patterns of error in individual student's papers. We often worked with non-native English speakers, and while that was tough, it enlightened me to the multiple ways in which each student needs help. While at PBSC, I have taught a developmental class, and now have a thorough understanding of the range of writing levels in which students enter the academic conversation.


An overarching theme that hovers over the entire play is Denmark's health as a nation being directly correlated to the actions and spirit of its ruling family. This theme ties into the other more in-your-face themes of the play, such as the lasting effects of the decisions a previous ruler has made 'haunting' the ruler next in line. Shakespeare wrote Hamlet in the 17th century after the Renaissance provided artists a new framework in which to work--and most importantly seek truth. Shakespeare's characters in Hamlet are all searching for truth in the intentions of others as nothing is as what it seems!
context: From document: Student Success Statement — chunk 5 (chunk 5/11)
